articles.headline: EOS-09 Satellite Fails Shortly After Launch, ISRO Initiates High-Level Inquiry
articles.explainer:
• India's Earth Observation Satellite EOS-09 experienced an anomaly shortly after lift-off from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, leading to mission failure.
• This incident marks a rare setback for the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) in recent years.
• An expert committee has been constituted to investigate the root cause of the failure, focusing on the launch vehicle and satellite systems.
• EOS-09 was intended for advanced earth imaging and environmental monitoring, crucial for agriculture, forestry, and disaster management.
• The failure raises questions about the reliability of the specific launch vehicle used and potential impacts on future missions.
gsSubTopics: Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: EOS-09 was an Earth Observation Satellite.
Launch took place from Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), Sriharikota.
ISRO is the primary space agency of India.
Previous successful EOS series satellites include EOS-01, EOS-02, EOS-03 (failed), EOS-04, EOS-06.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and national security.
Analyze the challenges faced by India's space program, including mission failures and technological hurdles.
Evaluate the implications of such failures on India's global standing in space technology and its commercial space ambitions.
Suggest measures to enhance the reliability and resilience of India's space launch capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a rocket launching from Satish Dhawan Space Centre with 'ISRO' logo.
articles.trivia: The Satish Dhawan Space Centre is located on Sriharikota, an island off the coast of Andhra Pradesh.

articles.headline: Five Killed in Bengaluru as Unprecedented Rains Wreak Havoc, Exposing Urban Infrastructure Gaps
articles.explainer:
• Heavy, unseasonal rainfall has led to severe urban flooding in Bengaluru, resulting in five fatalities due to rain-related incidents including wall collapses and electrocution.
• The deluge has paralyzed the city, causing widespread power outages, traffic congestion, and damage to property.
• Experts attribute the intensity of the rainfall to changing climate patterns and highlight the city's inadequate storm drain infrastructure and encroachment of lakes.
• Karnataka Chief Minister announced immediate relief measures and ordered a comprehensive review of urban planning and disaster preparedness.
• The incident reignites debate on sustainable urban development, climate resilience, and the need for robust civic infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Bengaluru is known as the 'Silicon Valley of India'.
Urban flooding is a common issue in many Indian cities.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) provides weather forecasts and warnings.
Climate change is increasing the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events.
mainsPoints: Examine the multi-faceted causes of urban flooding in Indian mega-cities like Bengaluru, including both natural and anthropogenic factors.
Discuss the role of climate change in exacerbating extreme weather events and their impact on urban centers.
Propose sustainable urban planning and infrastructure development strategies to enhance climate resilience and mitigate flood risks.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current disaster management frameworks in responding to urban natural calamities.
articles.imageDescription: A flooded street in Bengaluru with submerged vehicles and people wading through water.
articles.trivia: Bengaluru was formerly known as Bangalore and is the capital of Karnataka.

articles.headline: Kannada Writer Banu Mushtaq Wins 2025 International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp'
articles.explainer:
• Karnataka-based author Banu Mushtaq has been awarded the prestigious 2025 International Booker Prize for her short story anthology, 'Heart Lamp'.
• This marks a historic achievement as 'Heart Lamp' becomes the first Kannada language work to win the coveted international literary award.
• The book, translated into English by acclaimed translator Radha Devi, explores themes of identity, migration, and human resilience in contemporary India.
• The International Booker Prize celebrates the finest translated fiction from around the world, promoting global literature.
• Mushtaq's win is expected to significantly boost the visibility of regional Indian literature on the global stage and encourage more translations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization
articles.source: The Guardian / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Banu Mushtaq is a writer from Karnataka.
Her winning work is 'Heart Lamp', a short story anthology.
This is the first Kannada language work to win the International Booker Prize.
The International Booker Prize is awarded annually for a single book translated into English and published in the UK or Ireland.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of international literary awards like the Booker Prize in promoting regional Indian languages and literature globally.
Analyze the role of literary translations in fostering cultural understanding and cross-cultural dialogue.
Examine the challenges faced by regional language authors in gaining international recognition and how such awards can overcome these barriers.
Highlight the themes and narrative styles often found in contemporary Indian regional fiction, as exemplified by 'Heart Lamp'.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a book titled 'Heart Lamp' with a silhouette of a woman reading.
articles.trivia: The International Booker Prize was formerly known as the Man Booker International Prize.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Reinforces Right to Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling emphasized the delicate balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• While affirming the core principles of data minimization and consent, the Court directed the government to review certain provisions related to exemptions for state agencies.
• This judgment reinforces India's commitment to protecting digital privacy and sets a precedent for data governance in the digital age.
• The decision is expected to have significant implications for tech companies, government bodies, and individual citizens regarding data handling practices.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023 is India's primary law for data protection.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Puttaswamy judgment.
Data Fiduciary is an entity determining the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Data Protection Board of India is established under the Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and their potential impact on individual privacy and digital economy.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights, particularly the Right to Privacy, in the context of evolving technology.
Suggest reforms to further strengthen India's data governance framework, drawing lessons from international best practices.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books, symbolizing a Supreme Court ruling, with digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: The Justice B. N. Srikrishna Committee was instrumental in drafting India's initial data protection framework.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns and Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision comes as retail inflation, though moderating, remains above the MPC's comfort zone, coupled with global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted the need for continued vigilance on inflation and reiterated the commitment to achieving the 4% inflation target.
• The central bank also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a cautious approach to monetary policy in the near future.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts are unlikely until inflation shows a sustained downward trend and global economic stability improves.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
articles.source: Reserve Bank of India / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: The RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
India's retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth, considering both domestic and global factors.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation and external economic shocks.
Examine the potential implications of the current monetary policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing a stable interest rate line with the RBI logo in the background.
articles.trivia: The MPC was established in 2016 and comprises three members from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Renewed Focus on Maritime Security and Blue Economy Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint declaration was issued, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster relief.
• Key initiatives were launched to boost connectivity, trade, and investment, including a new framework for 'Blue Economy' collaboration.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its Act East Policy, strengthening strategic partnerships across Southeast Asia.
• Discussions also covered regional stability, supply chain resilience, and digital transformation, aiming for comprehensive strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
keyTerms: Code of Conduct (CoC) in South China Sea
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) comprises 10 member states.
India is a Dialogue Partner of ASEAN.
The Act East Policy is a successor to the Look East Policy.
The Indo-Pacific region is strategically significant for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in strengthening ties with ASEAN and ensuring regional stability.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in enhancing maritime security and promoting a 'Blue Economy' in the Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the role of ASEAN centrality in India's vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
Examine how increased connectivity and trade initiatives between India and ASEAN can contribute to regional economic integration and supply chain resilience.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member countries arranged around a globe, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: India Unveils New Comprehensive National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 for Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025, aiming for a robust circular economy.
• The policy introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms, mandating manufacturers to collect and recycle a higher percentage of their electronic waste.
• It emphasizes formalizing the informal e-waste sector, ensuring safe working conditions and environmental compliance for recyclers.
• Key features include a digital platform for tracking e-waste flow, incentives for certified recyclers, and public awareness campaigns on responsible disposal.
• This policy is crucial for mitigating environmental pollution, recovering valuable resources, and promoting sustainable consumption and production practices.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of Environment / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: E-waste is electronic waste, including discarded electrical and electronic equipment.
EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) makes producers responsible for the post-consumer stage of their products.
India is among the top e-waste generating countries globally.
The Basel Convention regulates transboundary movements of hazardous wastes and their disposal.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by rapidly increasing e-waste generation in India and its environmental and health implications.
Discuss the key features of the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 and evaluate its potential effectiveness in promoting a circular economy.
Examine the role of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in sustainable waste management and the need for formalizing the informal recycling sector.
Suggest additional policy measures and technological innovations required to achieve efficient and environmentally sound e-waste management in India.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, with a recycling symbol superimposed.
articles.trivia: India's first e-waste clinic was inaugurated in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh.

articles.headline: ISRO Forms High-Level Committee to Probe EOS-09 Launch Anomaly, Aims for Swift Course Correction
articles.explainer:
• Following the unsuccessful launch of the EOS-09 satellite, ISRO has promptly established a high-powered 'Anomaly Review Committee' (ARC).
• The committee, comprising veteran scientists and engineers, will conduct a detailed investigation into the technical failure of the launch vehicle and satellite systems.
• Its mandate includes identifying the precise cause, recommending corrective actions, and ensuring the reliability of future missions.
• ISRO Chairman stated that lessons learned from this incident will be crucial for enhancing India's space capabilities and mission success rates.
• Preliminary analysis suggests a potential issue in the second stage propulsion system, though a full report is awaited.
prelimsPoints: ISRO often forms expert committees to investigate mission failures.
Launch vehicles typically have multiple stages for propulsion.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre is ISRO's primary spaceport.
India's space program has a high success rate historically.
mainsPoints: Discuss the standard procedures and institutional mechanisms employed by ISRO to investigate and learn from mission failures.
Analyze the importance of transparency and accountability in national space programs, especially after setbacks.
Examine how technological advancements and rigorous testing protocols contribute to improving the reliability of space missions.
Highlight the resilience of India's space scientists in overcoming challenges and continuing to push the boundaries of space exploration.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists in a control room analyzing data on large screens.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Gramin Digital Kranti' Scheme to Bridge Rural-Urban Digital Divide
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has rolled out the 'Gramin Digital Kranti' (Rural Digital Revolution) scheme.
• Aimed at enhancing digital literacy and connectivity in rural areas, the scheme plans to provide high-speed internet access to all villages by 2027.
• It includes provisions for affordable digital devices, training programs for digital skills, and promotion of e-governance services at the Gram Panchayat level.
• The initiative seeks to empower rural communities, facilitate access to online education, healthcare, and financial services.
• This ambitious scheme is a crucial step towards achieving inclusive digital transformation and bridging the persistent rural-urban digital divide.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for digital initiatives.
BharatNet project aims to provide broadband connectivity to all Gram Panchayats.
Common Service Centres (CSCs) are access points for delivery of essential public utility services.
Digital literacy is the ability to find, evaluate, and compose information through digital tools.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of bridging the rural-urban digital divide for India's inclusive growth and socio-economic development.
Analyze the key components and objectives of the 'Gramin Digital Kranti' scheme and its potential impact on rural empowerment.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale digital infrastructure projects in rural areas, including geographical, financial, and human resource constraints.
Suggest policy interventions and public-private partnerships that can ensure the successful and equitable rollout of digital services in rural India.
articles.imageDescription: A rural Indian family using a tablet device, with a modern digital interface.
articles.trivia: The 'Digital India' programme was launched on 1 July 2015 to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.

articles.headline: Border Security Forces Step Up Vigilance Along Western Borders Amidst Emerging Threats
articles.explainer:
• Indian Border Security Forces (BSF) have intensified surveillance and patrolling along the western borders with Pakistan.
• The move follows intelligence reports of increased cross-border infiltration attempts and drone activities for smuggling arms and narcotics.
• Advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, anti-drone systems, and night vision devices, are being deployed to augment human vigilance.
• Senior security officials held a review meeting to assess the evolving threat landscape and strategize counter-measures.
• This heightened alert aims to thwart hostile activities and maintain peace and stability in the sensitive border regions.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
articles.source: Ministry of Home Affairs / Times Now
prelimsPoints: The BSF is India's primary border guarding organization for its borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.
The Line of Control (LoC) is a de facto boundary between India and Pakistan in Jammu & Kashmir.
Drones are increasingly used for surveillance and smuggling by hostile elements.
The Ministry of Home Affairs is responsible for internal security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of security threats along India's western borders, including cross-border terrorism, drone infiltration, and narcotics smuggling.
Discuss the role of technology, such as anti-drone systems and integrated surveillance, in enhancing border management and security.
Evaluate the challenges faced by border guarding forces in maintaining vigilance across diverse terrains and dealing with hybrid threats.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, encompassing diplomatic, intelligence, and operational measures, to ensure robust border security.
articles.imageDescription: BSF jawans patrolling a desert border area with surveillance equipment.
articles.trivia: The BSF was formed on 1 December 1965, in the wake of the 1965 Indo-Pakistan War.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Records Robust Growth in May, Indicating Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to 58.9 in May 2025, signaling a strong expansion in the sector.
• This robust growth is attributed to sustained demand, increased new orders, and improved business confidence among manufacturers.
• The rise in PMI indicates a healthy rebound in industrial activity, contributing significantly to overall economic recovery.
• Employment in the manufacturing sector also witnessed an uptick, suggesting positive trends in job creation.
• Economists view this strong PMI data as a key indicator of India's economic resilience amidst global economic uncertainties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
PMI is a composite index based on five major indicators: new orders, inventory levels, production, supplier deliveries, and the employment environment.
Manufacturing is a key sector for India's GDP and employment.
S&P Global compiles and releases PMI data for various countries.
mainsPoints: Explain the significance of the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) as a leading economic indicator and its implications for India's manufacturing sector.
Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's manufacturing PMI, including domestic demand and government policies.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining high growth in the manufacturing sector to achieve India's 'Make in India' objectives.
Evaluate the correlation between manufacturing growth, job creation, and overall economic stability in the Indian context.
articles.imageDescription: Factory floor with machinery in operation, symbolizing industrial growth.
articles.trivia: The first official PMI was introduced by the Institute for Supply Management (ISM) in 1931.

articles.headline: Tiger Population Shows Significant Increase in Western Ghats, Credit to Enhanced Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• A recent wildlife census has revealed a notable increase in the tiger population across various reserves in the Western Ghats region.
• This positive trend is attributed to sustained conservation efforts, including anti-poaching measures, habitat restoration, and community involvement.
• The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) highlighted the success of Project Tiger and the importance of continuous monitoring and protection.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is a critical tiger habitat and biodiversity hotspot.
• This success underscores India's commitment to wildlife preservation and achieving global conservation targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
India hosts over 70% of the world's tiger population.
Tiger census in India is conducted every four years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and a critical tiger habitat.
Analyze the key strategies and initiatives under Project Tiger that have contributed to the increase in tiger populations in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced in tiger conservation, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and poaching.
Suggest measures for sustainable wildlife management and community participation to ensure long-term conservation success.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest in the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Electoral Funding Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice has submitted its report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in electoral funding.
• Key recommendations include bringing all political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for financial disclosures.
• The panel also proposed capping corporate donations, strengthening the Election Commission's regulatory powers, and reviewing the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• These reforms aim to address concerns about opaque funding, potential quid pro quo arrangements, and the influence of money in politics.
• The report calls for a multi-party consensus to implement these changes, crucial for strengthening democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds were introduced in 2018.
The RTI Act, 2005 allows citizens to request information from public authorities.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an autonomous constitutional authority.
Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and propose amendments.
mainsPoints: Analyze the issues surrounding electoral funding in India, with a specific focus on transparency and accountability concerns.
Discuss the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel regarding electoral funding reforms, including the role of RTI and Electoral Bonds.
Evaluate the impact of opaque political funding on democratic processes, electoral integrity, and public trust.
Propose comprehensive electoral reforms that can ensure fair and transparent elections in India, drawing lessons from international practices.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing money in elections, next to a document labeled 'Reforms'.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights Alarming Global Food Insecurity, Calls for India's Leadership in Sustainable Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• A new United Nations report on global food security paints a grim picture, noting a significant rise in the number of people facing acute hunger worldwide.
• The report attributes the crisis to ongoing conflicts, climate change impacts, and economic downturns, exacerbated by supply chain disruptions.
• It specifically calls upon India, as a major agricultural producer and global leader, to play a more prominent role in ensuring global food security through sustainable practices.
• Recommendations include investing in climate-resilient agriculture, strengthening food supply chains, and promoting equitable food distribution.
• India's public distribution system and national food security initiatives were highlighted as potential models for other developing nations.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Issues relating to poverty and hunger
prelimsPoints: SDG 2 aims to achieve 'Zero Hunger' by 2030.
The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) is a specialized agency of the UN.
India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat.
The National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013 provides for food and nutritional security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional causes of global food insecurity and the specific challenges faced by developing nations.
Discuss India's potential and responsibilities as a major agricultural power in contributing to global food security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's domestic food security programs, such as the PDS, and their replicability in other contexts.
Suggest policy measures and international cooperation frameworks to promote sustainable agriculture and address hunger on a global scale.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with hands cupping a seedling, symbolizing food security and sustainability.
articles.trivia: The Green Revolution significantly boosted India's food grain production in the late 1960s.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Early Detection and Treatment of Alzheimer's Disease
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) and AIIMS have announced a significant breakthrough in Alzheimer's disease research.
• Their collaborative study identified a novel biomarker for early detection, enabling diagnosis years before clinical symptoms appear.
• Additionally, a new therapeutic compound has shown promising results in slowing down neural degeneration in preclinical trials.
• This advancement could revolutionize the management of Alzheimer's, offering hope for more effective interventions and improved patient outcomes.
• The research highlights India's growing capabilities in advanced medical science and neurodegenerative disease research.
gsSubTopics: Developments in Science and Technology and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Alzheimer's is a progressive neurodegenerative disease.
IISc (Indian Institute of Science) is a premier research institution.
AIIMS (All India Institute of Medical Sciences) is a group of public medical colleges.
Biomarkers are measurable indicators of the severity or presence of some disease state.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's in India and the challenges in their diagnosis and treatment.
Analyze the significance of identifying early biomarkers for diseases and its impact on public health interventions.
Evaluate India's advancements and potential in medical research, particularly in areas like neuroscience and biotechnology.
Suggest strategies for translating scientific breakthroughs into accessible and affordable healthcare solutions for the wider population.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized brain image with neural connections, symbolizing neurological research.
articles.trivia: Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of dementia.

articles.headline: National Survey Reveals Persistent High Youth Unemployment, Calls for Targeted Skilling Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship released its annual 'National Youth Unemployment Survey 2024-25', highlighting persistent high rates of youth unemployment.
• The survey indicates a significant mismatch between available skills and industry demands, particularly in emerging sectors.
• It also points to challenges in job creation, especially for graduates in traditional disciplines, and the need for vocational training.
• Recommendations include revamping skill development programs, promoting entrepreneurship, and fostering industry-academia collaboration.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for comprehensive policy interventions to harness India's demographic dividend effectively.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development / The Mint
prelimsPoints: India has a significant youth population, often referred to as its demographic dividend.
The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship oversees skill development initiatives.
Unemployment rate is the percentage of the labor force that is jobless.
The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) is a key source of employment data in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and consequences of high youth unemployment in India, considering both structural and cyclical factors.
Discuss the concept of 'demographic dividend' and how persistent unemployment can transform it into a demographic burden.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies aimed at skill development and employment generation for youth.
Propose comprehensive strategies, including educational reforms, industry collaboration, and entrepreneurship promotion, to address youth unemployment and harness India's human capital.
articles.imageDescription: Graduates in caps and gowns looking at a challenging economic landscape.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have the world's largest working-age population by 2030.

articles.headline: Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor Nears Completion, Set to Revolutionize Logistics and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), a flagship infrastructure project, is in its final stages of completion, with over 90% of the route operational.
• This 1,856 km long corridor connects Punjab to West Bengal, significantly enhancing freight movement efficiency and reducing transit times.
• The EDFC is expected to decongest existing railway lines, boost industrial growth in the corridor regions, and reduce logistics costs for businesses.
• It is a crucial component of India's national infrastructure pipeline, aimed at improving connectivity and fostering economic development.
• The project utilizes advanced signaling and electrification systems, positioning India's freight rail network among the most modern globally.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Ltd. / Business Today
prelimsPoints: The EDFC connects Ludhiana (Punjab) to Dankuni (West Bengal).
The Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) connects Dadri (Uttar Pradesh) to Jawaharlal Nehru Port (Maharashtra).
Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL) is responsible for planning and development.
DFCs are critical for faster, more efficient, and cost-effective movement of goods.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs) for India's logistics sector and industrial development.
Discuss how the completion of the Eastern DFC will impact regional economies, trade, and the overall competitiveness of Indian industries.
Evaluate the challenges faced during the implementation of large-scale infrastructure projects like DFCs, including land acquisition and funding.
Suggest future infrastructure development priorities that can complement the DFC network to create a truly integrated and efficient logistics ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: A long freight train moving rapidly on a dedicated track, with industrial buildings in the background.
articles.trivia: The concept of Dedicated Freight Corridors was first mooted in 2005.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for Climate Adaptation Funds at G7 Outreach Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India participated in an outreach session of the G7 Summit, reiterating its commitment to global climate action and sustainable development.
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi, during his address, pledged India's enhanced contribution to climate adaptation funds for vulnerable nations.
• He urged developed countries to fulfill their financial commitments under the Paris Agreement and facilitate technology transfer to developing economies.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the green transition, building climate resilience, and ensuring energy security for all.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR) in addressing climate change.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The G7 (Group of Seven) is an inter-governmental political forum consisting of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
India is not a member of G7 but is often invited to outreach sessions.
The Paris Agreement is a legally binding international treaty on climate change.
Climate adaptation refers to adjustments in natural or human systems in response to actual or expected climatic stimuli or their effects.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's stance on climate finance and technology transfer at international forums like the G7, emphasizing the principle of CBDR.
Discuss the importance of climate adaptation funds for vulnerable developing nations and the role of developed countries in fulfilling their commitments.
Evaluate India's initiatives and contributions towards achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
Examine the challenges in accelerating the global green transition and building climate resilience, considering geopolitical and economic factors.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from various countries gathered at a G7 summit table, with a climate graphic in the background.
articles.trivia: The G7 originated in 1975 as an informal forum for the heads of the world’s leading industrialized countries.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Khel Parampara' Initiative to Revive and Promote Traditional Indian Sports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports has unveiled 'Khel Parampara', a nationwide initiative aimed at reviving and popularizing traditional Indian sports.
• The program will focus on identifying, documenting, and promoting indigenous games like Mallakhamb, Kalaripayattu, Gatka, and Thang-Ta.
• It includes provisions for training academies, financial support for traditional sportspersons, and incorporating these games into school curricula.
• The initiative seeks to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, promote physical fitness, and create new avenues for sports talent.
• 'Khel Parampara' is expected to foster a sense of national pride and contribute to a healthier, more active population.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of sports in nation building
articles.source: Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports / PTI
prelimsPoints: Mallakhamb is a traditional Indian sport where a gymnast performs aerial yoga postures and wrestling grips in concert with a vertical stationary or hanging wooden pole.
Kalaripayattu is an Indian martial art from Kerala.
Gatka is a Sikh martial art.
Thang-Ta is a Manipuri martial art.
mainsPoints: Discuss the cultural and historical significance of traditional Indian sports and their potential for promoting national identity and physical well-being.

Evaluate the 'Khel Parampara' initiative's approach to integrating traditional sports into mainstream sports and educational frameworks.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Policy to Boost Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Across India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power has announced a new comprehensive policy aimed at accelerating the deployment of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure nationwide.
• The policy simplifies licensing and approval processes for setting up charging stations, offering subsidies and incentives for private players and public sector undertakings.
• It mandates the development of charging corridors along national highways and in major cities, ensuring accessibility for EV users.
• This move is critical for addressing range anxiety, a major barrier to EV adoption, and promoting sustainable mobility.
• The policy aligns with India's ambitious targets for reducing carbon emissions and transitioning to a green economy.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Power / Economic Times Auto
prelimsPoints: The FAME India scheme promotes manufacturing of electric and hybrid vehicle technology.
India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by 2070.
Lithium-ion batteries are commonly used in EVs.
The Ministry of Power is responsible for electricity infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges and opportunities in accelerating Electric Vehicle (EV) adoption in India, with a focus on charging infrastructure.
Discuss the key provisions of the new government policy for EV charging infrastructure and its potential impact on sustainable mobility.
Evaluate the role of government incentives, technological advancements, and public-private partnerships in building a robust EV ecosystem.
Examine how increased EV penetration contributes to India's climate action goals, energy security, and reduction in urban air pollution.
articles.imageDescription: An electric car being charged at a public charging station.
articles.trivia: The first electric car was developed in the 1830s.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Enhanced Security, Economic Ties in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, with both nations reaffirming their commitment to a special strategic and global partnership.
• Discussions focused on bolstering defense cooperation, including joint military exercises and technology transfers, to ensure regional security.
• Economic ties were a major agenda, with agreements signed to boost Japanese investment in India's infrastructure and manufacturing sectors.
• Both countries reiterated their shared vision for a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, countering aggressive unilateral actions.
• The dialogue also covered collaboration in emerging technologies, clean energy, and multilateral forums like the Quad.

Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests

Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.
Both countries hold annual summits and various ministerial dialogues.
The Indo-Pacific region is a key focus of their foreign policies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in the context of changing geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the key areas of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, technology, and regional security.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan collaboration in promoting a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific and addressing regional challenges.
Examine how bilateral ties can be further strengthened to enhance supply chain resilience, counter economic coercion, and foster sustainable development.

articles.headline: New Species of Freshwater Fish Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Richness
articles.explainer:
• Ichthyologists have announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of freshwater fish in the biodiversity-rich streams of the Western Ghats.
• The new species, named 'Garra mahanadiensis', belongs to the family Cyprinidae and exhibits unique morphological features.
• This discovery underscores the immense, yet underexplored, biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Researchers emphasize the urgent need for comprehensive surveys and enhanced conservation measures to protect these fragile ecosystems.
• The finding contributes to the global understanding of ichthyofauna and highlights India's natural heritage.
articles.source: Journal of Threatened Taxa / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
Cyprinidae is the largest family of freshwater fish.
Ichthyology is the branch of zoology that deals with fish.
Mahanadi is a major river in Eastern India, though this discovery is in the Western Ghats, potentially a tributary or a related watershed.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of new species discoveries, particularly in biodiversity hotspots like the Western Ghats.
Analyze the threats to freshwater ecosystems in India, including pollution, habitat degradation, and climate change.
Evaluate the current conservation policies and protected area networks in safeguarding India's aquatic biodiversity.
Suggest strategies for integrating scientific research, community participation, and policy interventions for effective biodiversity conservation.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of the newly discovered freshwater fish species, 'Garra mahanadiensis'.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

highlights: India's EOS-09 satellite fails shortly after launch from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, prompting an ISRO inquiry.
Five people are killed in Bengaluru due to severe urban flooding caused by unprecedented heavy rains, highlighting infrastructure deficiencies.
Kannada writer Banu Mushtaq makes history by winning the 2025 International Booker Prize for her short story anthology 'Heart Lamp'.
Supreme Court upholds major provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, reinforcing privacy rights while seeking review of state exemptions.
RBI maintains the repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time, citing persistent inflation concerns and global economic uncertainties.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the EOS-09 satellite launch failure:
1. EOS-09 was an Earth Observation Satellite launched by ISRO.
2. The launch took place from Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC).
3. The failure marks ISRO's first mission failure in over two decades.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: EOS-09 was indeed an Earth Observation Satellite. Statement 2 is incorrect: The launch took place from Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), Sriharikota, not VSSC. Statement 3 is incorrect: ISRO has had mission failures in recent years, for example, the EOS-03 mission in 2021.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, consider the following statements:
1. The Act defines a 'Data Fiduciary' as an entity that determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
2. The Right to Privacy, though not explicitly mentioned, was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
3. The Act aims to establish a Data Protection Board of India.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The DPDP Act, 2023 defines Data Fiduciary as an entity determining the purpose and means of processing personal data. The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Puttaswamy judgment. The Act also provides for the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the International Booker Prize is/are correct?
1. It is awarded annually for a single book translated into English and published in the UK or Ireland.
2. The 2025 award was won by Banu Mushtaq for her work 'Heart Lamp', making it the first Kannada language work to win.
3. The prize money is shared equally between the author and the translator.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The International Booker Prize is indeed awarded annually for a translated book published in the UK or Ireland. Banu Mushtaq's 'Heart Lamp' is the first Kannada work to win. The prize money is typically divided equally between the author and translator to acknowledge their dual contribution.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC):
1. The MPC is a six-member body responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate.
2. Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The current retail inflation in India is measured by the Wholesale Price Index (WPI).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The MPC is a six-member body, and its primary objective is price stability while considering growth. Statement 3 is incorrect. Retail inflation in India is primarily measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), not WPI.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following traditional Indian sports is NOT correctly matched with its origin/description?
(a) Mallakhamb: Gymnastic feats on a vertical wooden pole.
(b) Kalaripayattu: Martial art from Kerala.
(c) Gatka: Sikh martial art.
(d) Thang-Ta: Traditional wrestling from Uttar Pradesh.
options: (a) Mallakhamb: Gymnastic feats on a vertical wooden pole.
(d) Thang-Ta: Traditional wrestling from Uttar Pradesh.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Thang-Ta: Traditional wrestling from Uttar Pradesh.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Thang-Ta is a Manipuri martial art, not traditional wrestling from Uttar Pradesh. The other options are correctly matched.

mainsMocks.question: The recent urban flooding in Bengaluru highlights the critical challenges faced by Indian cities in managing rapid urbanization and climate change impacts. Analyze the multi-faceted causes of urban flooding and suggest comprehensive strategies for building climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Environmental pollution and degradation; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce urban flooding in Bengaluru as a case study. Discuss causes: intense rainfall (climate change), inadequate drainage, encroachment of water bodies, unplanned urbanization. Suggest strategies: integrated stormwater management, green infrastructure, land-use planning, early warning systems, community participation, legal enforcement, and climate-resilient construction.

mainsMocks.question: India's recent satellite launch failure and persistent high youth unemployment rates present significant challenges to its aspirations as a global power. Critically evaluate the implications of such setbacks on India's 'Science & Technology' and 'Demographic Dividend' narratives, and suggest policy measures to overcome these hurdles.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Space technology, Indian achievements in science & technology; GS1/GS2/GS3: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, unemployment, human resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Address both issues separately and then link them to national aspirations. For satellite failure: discuss impact on ISRO's reputation, commercial space, and future missions; suggest robust R&D, quality control. For unemployment: discuss skill gaps, job creation, demographic burden; suggest educational reforms, vocational training, entrepreneurship. Conclude by emphasizing resilient policy-making and long-term vision.

mainsMocks.question: The 'Gramin Digital Kranti' scheme and the new National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 represent India's commitment to inclusive digital transformation and sustainable development. Discuss the key features and objectives of these initiatives and evaluate their potential in bridging the digital divide and promoting a circular economy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions, Information Technology; GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, Waste Management, Circular Economy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce both initiatives. For 'Gramin Digital Kranti': explain goals (rural internet, digital literacy, e-governance), benefits (empowerment, access to services), and challenges. For E-Waste Policy: discuss EPR, formalizing informal sector, resource recovery, circular economy. Evaluate potential for both, highlighting synergies and necessary implementation safeguards. Conclude on integrated policy approach.

GS1.headline: Kannada Writer Banu Mushtaq Wins 2025 International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp'
GS1.explainer:
• Karnataka-based author Banu Mushtaq has been awarded the prestigious 2025 International Booker Prize for her short story anthology, 'Heart Lamp'.
• This marks a historic achievement as 'Heart Lamp' becomes the first Kannada language work to win the coveted international literary award.
• The book, translated into English by acclaimed translator Radha Devi, explores themes of identity, migration, and human resilience in contemporary India.
• The International Booker Prize celebrates the finest translated fiction from around the world, promoting global literature.
• Mushtaq's win is expected to significantly boost the visibility of regional Indian literature on the global stage and encourage more translations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Role of women and women’s organization
GS1.source: The Guardian / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Banu Mushtaq is a writer from Karnataka.
Her winning work is 'Heart Lamp', a short story anthology.
This is the first Kannada language work to win the International Booker Prize.
The International Booker Prize is awarded annually for a single book translated into English and published in the UK or Ireland.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of international literary awards like the Booker Prize in promoting regional Indian languages and literature globally.
Analyze the role of literary translations in fostering cultural understanding and cross-cultural dialogue.
Examine the challenges faced by regional language authors in gaining international recognition and how such awards can overcome these barriers.
Highlight the themes and narrative styles often found in contemporary Indian regional fiction, as exemplified by 'Heart Lamp'.
GS1.imageDescription: A stylized image of a book titled 'Heart Lamp' with a silhouette of a woman reading.
GS1.trivia: The International Booker Prize was formerly known as the Man Booker International Prize.

GS1.headline: National Survey Reveals Persistent High Youth Unemployment, Calls for Targeted Skilling Initiatives
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship released its annual 'National Youth Unemployment Survey 2024-25', highlighting persistent high rates of youth unemployment.
• The survey indicates a significant mismatch between available skills and industry demands, particularly in emerging sectors.
• It also points to challenges in job creation, especially for graduates in traditional disciplines, and the need for vocational training.
• Recommendations include revamping skill development programs, promoting entrepreneurship, and fostering industry-academia collaboration.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for comprehensive policy interventions to harness India's demographic dividend effectively.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
GS1.source: Ministry of Skill Development / The Mint

The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship oversees skill development initiatives.
Unemployment rate is the percentage of the labor force that is jobless.
The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) is a key source of employment data in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and consequences of high youth unemployment in India, considering both structural and cyclical factors.
Discuss the concept of 'demographic dividend' and how persistent unemployment can transform it into a demographic burden.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government schemes and policies aimed at skill development and employment generation for youth.
Propose comprehensive strategies, including educational reforms, industry collaboration, and entrepreneurship promotion, to address youth unemployment and harness India's human capital.

GS1.headline: Government Launches 'Khel Parampara' Initiative to Revive and Promote Traditional Indian Sports
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports has unveiled 'Khel Parampara', a nationwide initiative aimed at reviving and popularizing traditional Indian sports.
• The program will focus on identifying, documenting, and promoting indigenous games like Mallakhamb, Kalaripayattu, Gatka, and Thang-Ta.
• It includes provisions for training academies, financial support for traditional sportspersons, and incorporating these games into school curricula.
• The initiative seeks to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, promote physical fitness, and create new avenues for sports talent.
• 'Khel Parampara' is expected to foster a sense of national pride and contribute to a healthier, more active population.

Role of sports in nation building

Thang-Ta is a Manipuri martial art.

Analyze the challenges in preserving and promoting indigenous games in the modern era, including lack of infrastructure and public awareness.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Reinforces Right to Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling emphasized the delicate balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• While affirming the core principles of data minimization and consent, the Court directed the government to review certain provisions related to exemptions for state agencies.
• This judgment reinforces India's commitment to protecting digital privacy and sets a precedent for data governance in the digital age.
• The decision is expected to have significant implications for tech companies, government bodies, and individual citizens regarding data handling practices.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Act, 2023 is India's primary law for data protection.
The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 by the Puttaswamy judgment.
Data Fiduciary is an entity determining the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Data Protection Board of India is established under the Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and their potential impact on individual privacy and digital economy.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual data privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in interpreting and upholding fundamental rights, particularly the Right to Privacy, in the context of evolving technology.
Suggest reforms to further strengthen India's data governance framework, drawing lessons from international best practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and law books, symbolizing a Supreme Court ruling, with digital data streams in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Justice B. N. Srikrishna Committee was instrumental in drafting India's initial data protection framework.

GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint declaration was issued, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster relief.
• Key initiatives were launched to boost connectivity, trade, and investment, including a new framework for 'Blue Economy' collaboration.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its Act East Policy, strengthening strategic partnerships across Southeast Asia.
• Discussions also covered regional stability, supply chain resilience, and digital transformation, aiming for comprehensive strategic partnership.

The Indo-Pacific region is strategically significant for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in strengthening ties with ASEAN and ensuring regional stability.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in enhancing maritime security and promoting a 'Blue Economy' in the Indo-Pacific.

Examine how increased connectivity and trade initiatives between India and ASEAN can contribute to regional economic integration and supply chain resilience.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Gramin Digital Kranti' Scheme to Bridge Rural-Urban Digital Divide
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has rolled out the 'Gramin Digital Kranti' (Rural Digital Revolution) scheme.
• Aimed at enhancing digital literacy and connectivity in rural areas, the scheme plans to provide high-speed internet access to all villages by 2027.
• It includes provisions for affordable digital devices, training programs for digital skills, and promotion of e-governance services at the Gram Panchayat level.
• The initiative seeks to empower rural communities, facilitate access to online education, healthcare, and financial services.
• This ambitious scheme is a crucial step towards achieving inclusive digital transformation and bridging the persistent rural-urban digital divide.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Electoral Funding Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice has submitted its report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in electoral funding.
• Key recommendations include bringing all political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for financial disclosures.
• The panel also proposed capping corporate donations, strengthening the Election Commission's regulatory powers, and reviewing the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• These reforms aim to address concerns about opaque funding, potential quid pro quo arrangements, and the influence of money in politics.
• The report calls for a multi-party consensus to implement these changes, crucial for strengthening democratic processes.

The RTI Act, 2005 allows citizens to request information from public authorities.

Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and propose amendments.

Evaluate the impact of opaque political funding on democratic processes, electoral integrity, and public trust.
Propose comprehensive electoral reforms that can ensure fair and transparent elections in India, drawing lessons from international practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing money in elections, next to a document labeled 'Reforms'.

GS2.headline: UN Report Highlights Alarming Global Food Insecurity, Calls for India's Leadership in Sustainable Agriculture
GS2.explainer:
• A new United Nations report on global food security paints a grim picture, noting a significant rise in the number of people facing acute hunger worldwide.
• The report attributes the crisis to ongoing conflicts, climate change impacts, and economic downturns, exacerbated by supply chain disruptions.
• It specifically calls upon India, as a major agricultural producer and global leader, to play a more prominent role in ensuring global food security through sustainable practices.
• Recommendations include investing in climate-resilient agriculture, strengthening food supply chains, and promoting equitable food distribution.
• India's public distribution system and national food security initiatives were highlighted as potential models for other developing nations.

Suggest policy measures and international cooperation frameworks to promote sustainable agriculture and address hunger on a global scale.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Support for Climate Adaptation Funds at G7 Outreach Meeting
GS2.explainer:

• Prime Minister Narendra Modi, during his address, pledged India's enhanced contribution to climate adaptation funds for vulnerable nations.
• He urged developed countries to fulfill their financial commitments under the Paris Agreement and facilitate technology transfer to developing economies.
• Discussions focused on accelerating the green transition, building climate resilience, and ensuring energy security for all.
